body:
## Summary

The first play compiled on ADR 025's Level 3 model: a deterministic skeleton (pre-flight, one loop task, conditional checkpoint, gated close) with the loop's interior owned by the agent. Four walls keep the loop honest: a baked stop condition (`stop-condition.yaml`), the bundled scripts as the mechanical checker, an iteration cap of 5, and recorded evidence. Three defects harvested from prior sessions were designed out at the root rather than patched: (1) double-prefixed commit subjects — fixed by making the plan carry a bare subject and the executor own the conventional wrapper, with defensive normalization if an agent formats one anyway; (2) the play's own run artifacts left the tree dirty at hand-off — fixed by a final chore(stm) commit of the run-state directory as part of execution; (3) ignored paths caused mid-plan `git add` failures — fixed by a pre-check that turns an ignored path into a recorded exclusion before it can fail.

**SELF-TEST:** this PR is its own proof. Its commits were made by the recompiled play running against itself (`21be7bf` feat, `55ad31f` chore context, `9b3ead0` chore run-state, `f7cf82b` chore self-review). The loop converged in round 1, and the stop-condition verdict reads held for all four Done-means clauses (D1–D4) — recorded at `.garura/project/issues/465/status/stop-condition-commit-change.yaml`.

## Self-review checklist (full)

Rules source: `~/.garura/core/memory/standards/rules/self-review.md` (base, no project override).

**Scope checks**
- Matches the issue — PASS. Every changed file is either the recompiled play (`reference/ice.md`, `SKILL.md`, `scripts/execute_commits.py`, `scripts/check_stop_condition.py`, `stop-condition.yaml`) or #465's own STM evidence.
- No scope creep — PASS. No unrelated files, no drive-by edits to other plays.
- Reasonable size — PASS. 11 files, +558/-165, a full ICE-driven recompile with a 1:1 explaining ICE diff.
- No stray artifacts — PASS. No commented-out code, debug prints, or scratch files.

**Quality checks**
- Tests present — PASS (substitute form). No unit-test harness applies to a play artifact; the substitute proof is the self-test (play committed its own recompile, loop converged round 1, stop-condition held). Independently re-ran `play-editor/scripts/lint_play.py` against the compiled `SKILL.md` and confirmed 12/12 PASS (sections, F/S/C coverage, 1:1 recovery, no orphans, pipeline position, standard play close, pre-flight resolver, stop-condition manifest+checker, fingerprint, next-command). Both new scripts parse clean.
- Commits are clean — PASS. All commits conventional, each a coherent concern, each referencing #465, no doubled prefixes.
- No secrets — PASS. Diff grepped for credential/token/key patterns; only hits are documentation prose describing the scanner's own behavior. `analysis.yaml`'s risk scan reports empty sensitive_files.
- Docs in step — N/A. The play's compiled contract (`SKILL.md`/`ice.md`) is its own documentation, updated in the same commit; ADR 025 (already on main) is the design this pilot implements.
- Nothing obviously broken — PASS. No leftover TODOs; traced the new `normalize_subject`/`split_ignored`/run-state-dir logic against the ICE's C3/C4 extensions — matches. SKILL.md's step_evals/scenario_evals/recovery_entries counts (8/6/8) match the actual content.

**Verdict: PASS — 9/9 checks (8 PASS, 1 N/A). No FAIL.**

Closes #465

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Level 3 goal-loop pilot for review.

title: feat(components): commit-change recompiled as the Level 3 goal-loop pilot (#465)
